subUnits.title: 11.1 Converting Percentages to Decimals
notes: To convert a percentage to a decimal, divide by 100 (or move the decimal point two places to the left).
Always drop the '%' sign after converting.
Practice: Convert test scores, discount rates, and interest rates to decimals.

subUnits.title: 11.2 Percentage Difference: Estimated vs Actual Prices
notes: In real life, estimated prices often differ from actual market prices.
To find percentage difference: (Actual - Estimated) ÷ Actual × 100.
If estimated price is ₦4,000 and actual is ₦4,500, the difference is ₦500.
Percentage helps us understand how accurate our estimates are.
Useful for budgeting, shopping, and financial planning.

quiz.q: Estimated price: ₦200. Actual price: ₦250. What is the difference?
Why calculate percentage difference in prices?
opts: To measure estimation accuracy & adjust budgets
quiz.explanation: It shows how close your guess was to reality, helping improve future budgeting.

quiz.q: If actual price is ₦1,000 and you estimated ₦900, your estimate was:
